mechanism_narrative: Falling unemployment rate signals improving labor market conditions that boost household disposable income and consumer confidence, directly supporting corporate earnings and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC of UNRATE change vs 20-day forward returns is negative over any 2-year window, or t-test shows no significant positive mean return on months when UNRATE declines.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts exceeding 1.2 million units indicate robust residential construction activity that stimulates related industries and signals sustained domestic demand, leading to higher equity prices over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Over any 3-year period, conditional 60-day forward returns when HOUST > 1200 are not significantly positive (one-sided t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneously low federal funds rate and low consumer price inflation create an environment of accommodative real rates that reduces discount rates on equity cash flows and supports risk appetite, generating positive equity returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rolling 3-year correlation between (FEDFUNDS + CPIAUCSL) interaction term and forward 20-day returns is negative, or regime indicator yields Rank IC below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: During an economic expansion (defined by GDPC1 consistently above its prior peak), rising industrial production signals strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization that translates into higher corporate profits and positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-trading-day horizon.
failure_condition: In months classified as economic expansion, the 60-day forward return decile spread from INDPRO signals is negative or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real personal consumption expenditures reflect strong consumer demand that directly boosts revenues for consumer-facing firms, producing positive short-term S&P 500 returns within one week as the market prices in improved fundamentals.
failure_condition: Average 5-day forward return following an RSAFS increase is not positive or is inseparable from noise (Sharpe ratio < 0.2) over a full market cycle.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls to or below 5.0%, the labor market approaches full employment, increasing wage pressure and the likelihood of Fed tightening, which dampens equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In triggered periods (UNRATE <= 5.0), 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of instances over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in industrial production and nonfarm payrolls indicate synchronized real economic expansion across manufacturing and services, jointly confirming above-trend growth that lifts aggregate corporate earnings and pushes S&P 500 prices higher over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC of the interaction term (product of standardized INDPRO and PAYEMS changes) with 20-day forward returns is below 0.04 over any 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate has been at or below 0.25% for more than six consecutive months, the Federal Reserve's inability to raise rates signals deep concern about economic vitality, and the market interprets this as negative for growth expectations, depressing S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: In months where the trigger condition holds, average 60-day forward return is positive, or the signal has no significant negative rank correlation (|RankIC| < 0.03) over any 5-year window.
